parentQuestion: A patient wakes up unable to extend the wrist or fingers after falling asleep with their arm draped over the back of a chair. Which nerve is most likely compressed?

options: The wrist hanging limply because you can't extend it
scaffolds.explain: WRIST DROP is when the wrist HANGS LIMPLY because you can't EXTEND (lift) it. It's the classic sign of radial nerve injury — the extensors are paralyzed.

wrongExplains.0: Wrist drop is a medical sign of nerve damage, not an accessory.
wrongExplains.2: It's a pathological condition from nerve injury, not an exercise.
wrongExplains.3: It's a sign of nerve damage, not a treatment technique.

scaffolds.q: What does the radial nerve control?
options: All the muscles that EXTEND the wrist and fingers
scaffolds.explain: The RADIAL NERVE controls ALL the EXTENSOR MUSCLES on the back of the forearm. These muscles extend (straighten) the wrist and fingers. Damage it, and you get wrist drop.

wrongExplains.0: Leg muscles use lumbar and sacral plexus nerves. The radial is in the arm.
wrongExplains.2: Facial muscles use cranial nerve VII. The radial is for arm extension.
wrongExplains.3: Heart muscle is controlled by the autonomic system. The radial is for skeletal muscle.

scaffolds.q: Where is the radial nerve vulnerable to compression?
options: Where it wraps around the humerus in the spiral groove
scaffolds.explain: The radial nerve wraps around the back of the HUMERUS (upper arm bone) in the SPIRAL GROOVE. Here it's close to the bone and can be COMPRESSED against it.

wrongExplains.0: The nerve originates from the neck but is vulnerable at the spiral groove.
wrongExplains.2: The ankle has different nerves (tibial, fibular). The radial is in the arm.
wrongExplains.3: The chest isn't where the radial nerve is compressed in this scenario.

options: Radial nerve compression from sleeping with arm over a hard surface
scaffolds.explain: 'SATURDAY NIGHT PALSY' is radial nerve compression from sleeping with the arm over a chair (often while intoxicated). The name comes from people who don't shift position while sleeping.

wrongExplains.0: It's a medical condition named for when it typically occurs, not a dance.
wrongExplains.2: It's nerve damage from compression, not a party activity.
wrongExplains.3: It's an injury pattern, not an exercise routine.

scaffolds.q: How does sleeping with your arm over a chair cause nerve damage?
options: Prolonged pressure compresses the radial nerve against the bone
scaffolds.explain: Hours of PRESSURE on the arm compresses the radial nerve against the humerus in the spiral groove. This cuts off blood flow and squeezes the nerve fibers, causing damage.

wrongExplains.0: Objects don't get angry. The mechanism is physical pressure on the nerve.
wrongExplains.2: Temperature isn't the issue. Mechanical compression damages the nerve.
wrongExplains.3: Muscles don't grow during sleep. Nerve compression is the problem.

scaffolds.q: What movements are lost with radial nerve injury?
scaffolds.explain: Radial nerve injury causes loss of WRIST EXTENSION (can't lift wrist) and FINGER EXTENSION (can't straighten fingers). The wrist and fingers hang in flexion — classic wrist drop.

wrongExplains.0: Wrist flexion uses the median and ulnar nerves. Radial controls extension.
wrongExplains.2: Leg movement uses lumbar/sacral plexus nerves. The radial is in the arm.
wrongExplains.3: Facial expression uses cranial nerve VII. The radial controls arm extensors.

scaffolds.q: Why are the finger flexors still working if the radial nerve is damaged?
options: Flexors are controlled by the median and ulnar nerves, not the radial
All hand muscles use the radial nerve
scaffolds.explain: The MEDIAN and ULNAR nerves control finger FLEXION. The RADIAL nerve only controls EXTENSION. So with radial damage, you can still make a fist — you just can't open your hand.

wrongExplains.0: Flexors are definitely real muscles on the front of the forearm.
wrongExplains.2: Different nerves control different muscle groups. Radial is for extensors only.
wrongExplains.3: Flexor muscles definitely exist and are essential for grip.

scaffolds.q: Do most cases of Saturday night palsy recover?
options: Yes, most cases recover in weeks to months
scaffolds.explain: Most cases of Saturday night palsy are NEUROPRAXIA (temporary conduction block) and RECOVER in WEEKS TO MONTHS. Severe cases with axon damage take longer or may have permanent deficits.

wrongExplains.0: Most compression injuries recover. Only severe cases have permanent damage.
wrongExplains.2: Most cases recover without surgery. Surgery is rarely needed.
wrongExplains.3: Adults can recover too. Age isn't the primary factor in recovery.

scaffolds.q: Which nerve is compressed when someone wakes up with wrist drop after sleeping with arm over a chair?
scaffolds.explain: The RADIAL NERVE! It wraps around the humerus in the spiral groove where prolonged compression causes wrist drop. Loss of wrist and finger extension = radial nerve injury.

wrongExplains.0: The median nerve controls flexion and thumb opposition. Wrist drop indicates radial damage.
wrongExplains.1: The ulnar nerve controls hand intrinsics. Wrist drop indicates radial damage.
wrongExplains.3: The sciatic nerve is in the leg. This arm symptom is from radial nerve compression.
